sections.paragraph:
εὐθυδρομέω 1 aor. εὐθυδρόμησα of a ship run a straight course (s. εὐθύς, δρόμος; Philo, Agr. 174 πνεῦμα εὐθυδρομοῦν τὸ σκάφος ἀνέωσεν, Leg. All. 3, 223 ναῦς εὐθυδρομεῖ) εἰς Σαμοθράκην Ac 16:11 (w. εἰς as Agathias Hist. 2, 21 [VI a.d.]: HGM II 71). Abs. (Laud. Therap. 28 εὐθυδρομήσας διέφυγεν) εὐθυδρομήσαντες ἤλθομεν we ran a straight course and came 21:1 (εὐθυδρόμος in Polyb. 34, 4, 5; Orph. Hymns 21, 10 Qu.).—DELG s.v. δραμεῖν.

aor. aor. = aorist

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

Agathias Agathias , VI a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

HGM HGM = Historici Graecae Minores—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Orph. Hymns Orph. Hymns = Orphic Hymns, s. Orphica

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 406
